evidence_grants.title: Identification and characterization of novel antifungals against Cryptococcus spp
evidence_grants.abstract:
Cryptococcus neoformans and Cryptococcus gattii are major fungal pathogens that cause severe infections,
including cryptococcal meningitis, particularly in immunocompromised individuals. The World Health
Organization (WHO) has identified C. neoformans as one of the top four highest critical priority fungal
pathogens due to its substantial disease burden, increasing drug resistance, and the lack of effective therapies.
Even with available treatments, the mortality rate for cryptococcal meningitis

evidence_grants.title: AI-enhanced LVSim2.0 technology for rapid phenotypic susceptibility testing of new and experimental TB drugs
evidence_grants.abstract:
PROJECT SUMMARY
Tuberculosis (TB) and multidrug-resistant TB (MDR-TB) remain major global health threats, with over 10 million
cases and 1.5 million deaths annually. MDR-TB presents a significant barrier to TB control, with nearly 20% of
affected patients dying within a year of treatment initiation. Accurate and timely drug susceptibility testing (DST)
is essential to guide therapy and prevent ineffective treatment, yet current DST methods are insufficient.
Molecular resistance assays, though ra

institution: GRADUATE SCHOOL OF PUBLIC HEALTH AND HEALTH POLICY

evidence_grants.title: Proyecto VIDA (VIH Información Distribución y Acción): A Longitudinal Study of HIV Incidence and Engagement in HIV Prevention and Care Among MSM in Colombia
evidence_grants.abstract:
SUMMARY
RFA-AI-24-009 (LITE-LA) is designed to address critical needs in HIV prevention research by using innovative
technology to enroll and follow large-scale epidemiological cohorts of those at highest risk of infection in Latin
America. In response to the LITE-LA RFA, our proposed study (Proyecto VIDA) will replicate established Internet-
based strategies—employed successfully by the research team in LITE1 and LITE2—to enroll a new cohort of
5,000 HIV-negative men who have sex with men (MSM)

evidence_grants.title: 2026 Annual Meeting of the American Society of Pharmacognosy: Elevating Natural Products
evidence_grants.abstract:
Project summary
The 2026 Annual Meeting of the American Society of Pharmacognosy (ASP) will be held July 26–29, 2026, at
the Denver Tech Center Marriott in Denver, Colorado. This multidisciplinary conference will convene scientists
from academia, government, and industry to advance the accelerated discovery, characterization, and
translational application of natural products. Natural products are small organic molecules produced by living
organisms that play critical roles in biological systems.

evidence_grants.title: Medical Artificial Intelligence Core (MEDAI Core)
evidence_grants.abstract: PROJECT SUMMARY – A Medical AI core is proposed to capitalize on the unprecedented opportunities presented by recent breakthroughs in artificial intelligence and machine learning (AI/ML) to accelerate biomedical discoveries. Bringing together computer scientists and biomedical data scientists with broad expertise, the MEDAI Core’s objective will be to increase the effectiveness of biomedical researchers by helping them leverage data-driven AI/ML methods. To achieve this objective, the MEDAI Core

evidence_grants.title: Project I - Exploring use of Full Body Two-Dimensional Photography Combined with Artificial Intelligence to Triage Skin Lesions
evidence_grants.abstract:
PROJECT SUMMARY – Project 1 
Regardless of skin complexion, skin cancer remains the most commonly diagnosed cancer and the only cancer type where incidence rates are rising. More than 90% of melanomas can be visually diagnosed. However, melanoma diagnosis remains troublesome, as the current diagnostic criteria are subjective and imprecise. This poor diagnostic precision adds to the 1.6 billion dollars in annual therapeutic costs for melanoma. Artificial intelligence (AI) using computer vision (C

evidence_grants.title: Project IV - Isolating placental effects of environmental toxicants in fetal development: AI/ML-augmented multi-omic causal discovery and prognostic modeling
evidence_grants.abstract:
Project Summary – Project 4 
Exposure to per- / poly-fluoroalkyl substances (PFAS), a large class of persistent, pervasive, and 
physiologically active, environmental contaminants, are a major potential source of adverse pregnancy and 
child health outcomes. However, the role of the placenta in regulating exposure to and effects of these 
chemicals is not well studied. Past research has been limited in two major ways: (a) Methods to quantify 
placental function have been too broad relying on est

evidence_grants.title: Herpes Simplex Immunotherapeutic Targeting CD8 T cells
evidence_grants.abstract:
Project Summary
Genital herpes (GH) is caused by herpes simplex virus (HSV) infection of epithelial cells, which leads to
permanent infection of neurons in anatomically connected ganglia and then periodic recurrences with genital
lesions, pain, live virus shedding, and potential transmission. Currently, GH is incurable. Responding to RFA-
AI-24-068 “New Therapeutic Strategies for Genital Herpes”, we propose the optimization and advanced pre-
clinical development of an immunotherapeutic to boost

evidence_grants.title: Pacific Center for Artificial Intelligence and Data Science in Medicine (PAC-AID)
evidence_grants.abstract:
PROJECT SUMMARY – Overall
It is predicted that virtually every type of clinician will use data science, such as artificial intelligence (AI) and machine learning (ML), in the future. Further, AI/ML is expected to play a dominant role across medical disciplines throughout the human lifespan. The utilization and ultimate training of deep learning models require specialized education and skills. The curation and processing of large datasets from locally sourced patient populations requires a curati

evidence_grants.title: Core A: Administrative Core
evidence_grants.abstract:
PROJECT SUMMARY – Admin Core
The Administrative Core will be responsible for overseeing the establishment of the Pacific Center for
Artificial Intelligence and Data Science in Medicine (PAC-AID) COBRE at the University of Hawaii at
Manoa (UHM) and ensuring its overall success, growth, and sustainability. Consisting of the COBRE Directors,
Program Coordinator, Executive Committee, and Mentoring Teams, the Administrative (Admin) Core will
provide comprehensive logistical and administrative support

evidence_grants.title: Alteration and Renovation
evidence_grants.abstract:
PROJECT SUMMARY – Alterations and Renovations
The University of Hawai`i Cancer Center (UHCC) and the John A Burns School of Medicine are committed to
advancing its capabilities in data science and machine learning to address critical biomedical challenges in
Hawai`i and beyond. This proposal seeks to renovate and expand the UHCC data center to create a state-of-
the-art facility capable of supporting next-generation high-performance computing (HPC) systems. The current
infrastructure, designed b

evidence_grants.title: Project II - Leveraging artificial intelligence for integrative multiomics analysis of high-risk populations to understand the biologic etiology of pancreatic cancer
evidence_grants.abstract:
PROJECT SUMMARY – Project 2 
Project 2 Pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma (PDA) is a leading cause of cancer-related mortality in the United States, with rising incidence and death rates. Mortality rates vary across patient populations, and in those at the highest risk of developing PDA, mortality remains elevated even after accounting for established risk factors and socioeconomic measures. This pattern suggests a possible biological contribution. However, high-risk patient populations for which

evidence_grants.title: Project III - Systems Functional Genomics Mechanisms of Congenital Heart Diseases
evidence_grants.abstract:
PROJECT SUMMARY/ABSTRACT – Project 3 
Our long-term research goal is to improve disease prevention and intervention by integrating functional genomics/bioinformatics and data sciences to enhance biomedical research, focusing on congenital heart disease/defect (CHD). CHD is a major cause of early mortality and lifelong disability, primarily influenced by genetic and environmental factors, with incidence that varies across population characteristics. The cellular and molecular factors and mechanis

evidence_grants.title: Transcriptional Regulation of the Microbiota-Nutrition Axis in Genetically Diverse Macrophages During Psoriasiform Inflammation.
evidence_grants.abstract:
Project Summary
 Psoriasis is a chronic systemic inflammatory disease that affects approximately 100 million people
globally. It arises from complex interactions between polygenic host susceptibilities and environmental factors
like diet and the microbiome. While many genetic psoriasis susceptibility loci have been identified in Western
populations, some of these have not been replicated in Asian populations, underscoring the need to study the
effects of genetic variability on psoriasis. Importa

evidence_grants.title: Open-source Low-field MRI: Learn, Design, Build, Scan
evidence_grants.abstract:
SUMMARY
We propose a three-day workshop, “Open-source Low-field MRI: Learn, Build, Scan,” to train biomedical imaging
scientists through hands-on construction, scanning, and AI-based image reconstruction using low-cost, open-
source MRI systems. Building on the successful “DELTA DIY MRI” at Johns Hopkins, it supports NIBIB’s
leadership in biomedical imaging and NINDS’s neurotechnology workforce goals. The workshop’s three
phases—Learn, Design & Build, and Scan—combine lectures, hardware prototyp

evidence_grants.title: Naturally Occurring Canine Hemangiosarcoma as a Translational Model for Angiosarcoma
evidence_grants.abstract:
Angiosarcomas are malignant vascular cancers that are rare in humans, accounting for 1-2% of all sarcomas.
These aggressive malignancies have a high metastatic propensity, leading to a poor prognosis. No effective
targeted therapy for angiosarcomas has been established yet, with previous studies showing unfavorable
clinical benefits. The rarity of angiosarcoma and its underrepresentation in cancer research underscore the
urgent need for foundational studies and the development of reliable transl

evidence_grants.title: Artificial Intelligence-powered Advanced Ultrasound for Early Diagnosis of Intraocular Melanoma
evidence_grants.abstract:
SUMMARY
Choroidal melanoma is the most common primary intraocular malignancy in adults and a potentially fatal cancer
that continues to be challenging in all respects. Choroidal melanoma can arise from preexisting choroidal nevus
or de novo. Distinguishing early-stage choroidal melanoma from nevus can be challenging as they share similar
ophthalmoscopic appearances when small. Up now, no single objective gold standard modality can successfully
discriminate between these two entities. Formation o

evidence_grants.title: Quantitative point-of-care MRI for breast cancer screening
evidence_grants.abstract:
PROJECT SUMMARY
Breast cancer is the second leading cause of cancer-related death for women in the US. Three imaging
modalities are used for breast cancer screening – mammography is the standard routine imaging modality, while
ultrasound and MRI are reserved for supplemental imaging. Mammography has reduced breast cancer-specific
mortality in randomized controlled trials; however, there remains controversy regarding its high false-positive
outcomes, use of ionizing radiation, and low sensitivity

evidence_grants.title: Voice-activated and touchscreen-controlled smart speaker intervention for adults aged 50 years and older with poorly controlled type 2 diabetes
evidence_grants.abstract: Adults aged 50 years and older experience a disproportionate burden of diabetes, with nearly double the prevalence, greater risk of complications, and being twice as likely to die, compared to younger adults. Further compounding diabetes burden for older adults are unmet basic needs such as food insecurity, transportation barriers, utility needs, social isolation, and loneliness. These factors, combined with complex treatment regimens, create significant challenges for older adults with type 2 d

evidence_grants.title: MRI-based Quantification of Perfusion and Microstructure
evidence_grants.abstract:
PROJECT SUMMARY
 The overall goal of this application is to develop next-generation, confounder-corrected magnetic resonance
imaging (MRI)-based intra-voxel incoherent motion (IVIM) methods for simultaneous quantification of blood
perfusion and tissue microstructure. IVIM is a diffusion MRI-based technique that probes various types of
intra-voxel molecular motion without the need for ionizing radiation or exogenous contrast agents and with
enormous potential in diffuse and focal disease. Despite
